title: Withdraw 1,684,050 ada for Tx3 by TxPipe: Open API Layer for Cardano's dApp P...

rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Decisive reason: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal states there is currently no common interface mechanism across the Cardano ecosystem, forcing integrators to relearn each protocol and raising integration costs." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 1,308,000 ADA plus a 327,000 ADA contingency reserve over 12 months, with a total withdrawal of 1,684,050 ADA including a 49,050 ADA Intersect administration fee." and clears the evidence gates. What would change this vote to YES: Provide sustainability path; Provide cost-benefit clarity. Residual uncertainty: Treasury flow signal is in stressed regime (total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals). Confidence context: score -0.12; confidence 75.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: DRep ratification support is material but below threshold; treated as a modest context signal.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Withdraw treasury funds for Tx3 by TxPipe, an open API layer for Cardano dApp protocols with developer and agent-facing infrastructure.
Recipient: 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v, submitted by Intersect on behalf of the vendor TxPipe

Deliverables: Open, standardized interface across Cardano on-chain protocols, 12 additional protocol onboardings over 12 months, three per quarter, Developer and agent coverage for onboarded protocols, MCP server for agents to discover and call published protocols, Protocol skills packaging operations, Agent-first documentation and LLM-structured documentation, Auto-generated SDKs in multiple languages and live JSON-RPC endpoints

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 1,308,000 ADA plus a 327,000 ADA contingency reserve over 12 months, with a total withdrawal of 1,684,050 ADA including a 49,050 ADA Intersect administration fee.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Tx3 is intended to provide an open, standardized interface for Cardano protocols through documentation, multi-language SDKs, and live JSON-RPC endpoints.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states there is currently no common interface mechanism across the Cardano ecosystem, forcing integrators to relearn each protocol and raising integration costs.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposer claims AI agents will increase on-chain activity and require shared, auditable, deterministic protocol interfaces to avoid irreversible mistakes from hallucinated transactions.

Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Through Catalyst Fund 14, TxPipe has already put the developer layer into production with five protocols live on the Tx3 Protocol Registry at tx3.land.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal funds 12 additional protocol onboardings, three per quarter, plus development of the MCP server and skill framework as shared infrastructure for registry protocols.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal states there is currently no common interface mechanism across the Cardano ecosystem, forcing integrators to relearn each protocol and raising integration costs.

Independent evidence for: The proposer claims AI agents will increase on-chain activity and require shared, auditable, deterministic protocol interfaces to avoid irreversible mistakes from hallucinated transactions.
Independent evidence for: TxPipe has received funding through 30 Project Catalyst proposals from Funds 9 through 14, with 26 delivered and four currently under development and on schedule.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 1,308,000 ADA plus a 327,000 ADA contingency reserve over 12 months, with a total withdrawal of 1,684,050 ADA including a 49,050 ADA Intersect administration fee." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal states there is currently no common interface mechanism across the Cardano ecosystem, forcing integrators to relearn each protocol and raising integration costs." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

actionable_asks: State the cost against the benefit: what does this buy, per ada spent.
Describe the funding path beyond this grant, or say plainly that there isn't one.
Disclose the rate basis — headcount, FTE fraction, or day rate — so the price can be checked against the market.
Tie payment to defined deliverables, not to elapsed time or headcount.
State your AI-cost assumption: this work is cheaper to produce than it was, and the price should show it.
affirmative_waste_evidence: independently verified excessive cost versus market comparables
missing: cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

flags.detail: Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, which won a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.
